title: Rukmini V O vs Chandrashekr B on 20 January, 2025
body: Rukmini V O vs Chandrashekr B on 20 January, 2025 1 O.S.872/22 KABC010034602022 Presented on : 03-02-2022 Registered on : 03-02-2022 C.R.P.67 Govt. of Karnataka Form No.9(Civil) Title Sheet for Judgment in Suits (R.P.91) TITLE SHEET FOR JUDGMENTS IN SUITS IN THE COURT OF THE XII ADDL. CITY CIVIL JUDGE, AT BENGALURU CITY PRESENT: SRI. GANGADHARA.K.N., B.A.,LLM., XII ADDL.CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE (CCH.No.27), BENGALURU Dated this the 20th day of January, 2025. O.S.No.872/2022 PLAINTIFF : 1. MRS. V.O.RUKMINI W/o. M.R.Rajagopal Aged about 60 years. 2. Mr. M.R.RAJAGOPAL S/o. Late M.Ramappa, Aged about 65 years. Both are R/at. No.45, 1st cross, Basaveshwara Layout, Near Reddy Farm Road, BEML 3rd stage, 2 O.S.872/22 Rajarajeshwarinagar, Bengaluru-98. (By Sri.RN, Advocate) V/S DEFENDANTS : 1.Mr. B. CHANDRASHEKAR Aged about 65 years, S/o. Late G.H.Bangera. R/at. No.12/1, 2nd main, 9th cross Chamarajpet, Bengaluru-18. 2. Mrs. MANGALA GOWRI Aged about 60 years, D/o. Late G.H.Bangera, W/o. Raghuram, R/at. No.8/1, 4th main, 9th cross, Ramachandra Agrahara, Chamarajpet, Bengaluru-18. 3. Mr. B.UTTAM KUMAR Aged about 57 years, S/o. Late G.H.Bangera, R/at. No.404, 9th cross, 1st main road, Panchasheelanagar, Moodalapalya, Bengaluru-72. 4. Mrs. SUJATHA.B, Aged about 56 years, D/o. Late G.H.Bangera, W/o. Kumar, R/at. C/o. Tanuja Srinivas, Doddagubbi village, Bidarahalli Hobli, Bengaluru East Taluk. 5. Mr. H.SHARATH KUMAR Aged about 45 years, S/o. Late Harishchandra, grand S/o. Late 3 O.S.872/22 G.H.Bangera. 6. Mr. H. PUNEETHA KUMAR Aged about 45 years, S/o. Late Harishchandra, Grand S/o. Late G.H.Bangera. 7. Mr.H.RASHMI Aged about 41 years, D/o. Late Harishchandra, Grand s/o. Late G.H.Bangera. 8. Mr. H.RAJESH Aged about 39 years, S/o. Late Harishchandra Grand S/o. Late G.H.Bangera. 9. Mrs. H.SUREKHA Aged about 45 years, D/o. Late Harishchandra Grand D/o. Late G.H.Bangera Defendants 5 to 9 are residing at No.45, Periyanna Agrahara, Naidu layout, Rajivgandhi Road, Kanakapura Main Road, Bengaluru-72. (By Sri.CR - D1,2,4 D3 - Exparte, D5 - 9 Sri. AAN Advocate) Date of Institution of the : 03-02-2022 suit Nature of the suit : Injunction Date of commencement of : 20-06-2023 recording of the evidence 4 O.S.872/22 Date on which the : Judgment was 20-01-2025 pronounced Total Duration Years Months Days 02 11 17 (GANGADHARA.K.N.) XII ADDL.CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE BENGALURU CITY. JUDGMENT The Plaintiffs have filed the present suit for permanent injunction restraining the defendants, agents, anybody acting through them from coming in the way of the construction activities of the plaintiffs or making any efforts to trespass to delay the construction activity, so as to disturb the physical possession of the scheduled property of the plaintiffs. 2. Brief facts of the plaintiff's case is as follows:- Plaintiffs are the joint owners of the vacant site bearing No.18, formed and converted land bearing survey number 66/1, renumbered as 284/11, CMC Khata No.5140/284/11, present Municipal No.18/9/2 of Halagevaderahalli village, Kengeri Hobli, Bangalore South Taluk. Presently the said layout is known as 5 O.S.872/22 Giridhama Layout. The plaintiffs property is specifically described in the plaint. They contended that, originally one G.H.Bangera purchased the land in Sy.No.66/1 measuring 2 acres 04 guntas under a registered sale deed dated 16 th April 1971. He got the said land converted for residential purpose vide order dated 25 th April 1990 and he gave a GPA in favour of one S.M.Bansali to form the layout and to alienate the said sites. Accordingly, Said S.M.Bansali being the GPA holder of land lord G.H.Bangera sold the suit schedule property in favour of one Mrs. R.Sumitra under a registered sale deed dated 3rd July 1997. The said R.Sumitra got the Katha from then CMC and even paid the betterment charges on 5th January 2004. On 18th July 1997, said G.H.Bangera passed away. 3. Said Smt. R. Sumitra sold the suit scheduled property in favour of one Mrs.Yamuna under a registered Sale deed dated 9 th February 2004. Said Yamuna got the Katha in her name and was paying taxes. She sold the said property in favor of the plaintiffs herein under registered sale deed dated 27 th January 2011. After purchase of said property the plaintiffs have got the katha in their name to put up the construction, have obtained the plan under LP.No.334/2020-21. When the plaintiffs have commenced the 6 O.S.872/22 construction on 1st February 2022, the defendants came near the Suit schedule property, made an attempt to disturb the construction activity by showing the fact that they have filed suit against one Smt. Sumitra.R in OS.No.6907/2012. In the said suit, even the plaintiffs have got impleaded as defendants. In the said suit, they have not sought any relief against the plaintiffs herein. Thus, the plaintiff contended that they have every right to enjoy their property peacefully, but the defendants were claiming that they are the children and grandchildren of Original Landlord G.H.Bangera & are making an attempt to interfere with the construction activities. In fact, they are filed the false litigation with intent to blackmailing the bonafide purchasers and they are interfering with the plaintiff's construction activities, due to which causing a delay to complete the construction. Despite showing the fact that the plaintiffs have validly got purchased the property but defendants are not ready to allow the plaintiffs to put up the construction. Though, the plaintiffs claims to have approached the jurisdictional police by seeking protection, but who have advised them to approach the civil court for the reason that suit is a civil in nature. Thus, the plaintiffs contended that there was a cause of action to file the present suit. 7 O.S.872/22 4. Along with the suit, the plaintiff also filed IA No.1 under Order 39, Rule 1 and 2 of CPC by seeking an interim temporary injunction order to restrain the defendants herein from interfering with their possession and enjoyment, by considering the prima facie case made out by the plaintiffs this court was pleased to grant an interim injunction. The same is operating even today. There are nine defendants in the suit, D3 was placed exparte, the defendant Nos.1, 2, 4 and 5 to 9 have appeared through their counsels. Defendant no.1 and 4 filed their written statement and even defendant No. 5 to 9 have filed their separate written statement. 5. Defendants in their written statement contended that they admits that it is G.H.Bangera got purchased the land bearing Sy.No.66/1 measuring 2 acres, 4 guntas under a sale deed dated 16th April 1971. But denied of giving of GPA in favour of one S.M.Bansali to form layout and sell the sites carved therein and also denied of sale of suit site in favour of one Smt.R.Sumitra by a GPA holder of original landlord and in turn sale of said site by R. Sumitra in favor of one Smt.Yamuna and sale of said site in favor of 8 O.S.872/ 22 the plaintiffs and also denied the plaintiffs claim that they have got the building plan approved from the competent authority to put up construction. The defendant Nos.5 to 9 have contended that their grandfather, late G.H.Bangera by using the joint family funds had acquired the land bearing survey number 98/3, measuring to an extent of 23 Guntas, Survey No. 98/1 measuring to an extent of 1 acre 23 Guntas and Survey No. 67 measuring to an extent of 4 acres and Survey No. 66/1 measuring to an extent of 2 acres 4 Guntas of Halagevaderahalli village, Kengeri Hobli, Bangalore South Taluk, Bangalore. Said G.H.Bangera is none other than father of the defendant Nos.1 to 4 and grandfather of the defendant numbers 5 to 9. The registered Sale deed dated 16 th April 1971, based on which mutation got affected in his name. Thereafter, it is the defendant Nos.1 to 4 and mother of the defendant Nos.5 to 9 were personally cultivating the said properties. Thereafter, G.H. Bangera got the land converted by the order dated 25 th April 1990, but due to illness he died on 16th July 1997. After his demise, the defendant Nos.1 to 5 and mother of the defendant No.5 to 9, became an absolute owner of all the properties. There was a dispute among the legal heirs of G.H.Bangera in respect of Joint family properties. Thus, mother of the defendant Nos. 5 to 9 Smt 9 O.S.872/ 22 Hemavathy.B and Sri.B. Chandrasekhar have jointly instituted OS.NO.5442/2009 in which they have amicably resolved their dispute. Under which mother of the defendant Nos.5 to 9 has got 15 Guntas of land in survey No.66/1 as per MR.No.8/2010-2011. When matter was stood thus, Smt.Sumitra and others have came near property of deceased Smt. Hemavathi on 31 st August 2012 with some goonda elements, tried to evict their mother from the property bearing survey No.66/1 measuring 0.15.13.00 guntas, claiming that they were the owners of site formed in the said land and who claims to have got purchased the said site through G.P.A holder of one G.H.Bangera named S.M.Bansali, for which the mother of the defendant Nos.5 to 9 instituted suit for declaration in OS.No.6907/ 2012. In which Smt. Sumitra contended that she got purchased the suit property from the G.P.A. holder of G.H. Bangera under a sale deed dated 3rd July 1997. But this defendant denied that no GPA had been executed by G.H. Bangera in favor of anybody. The GPA is a created document. The legal heirs of G.H.Bangera claims to have registered a criminal case against the S.M.Bansali by filing PCR No.24688/2011. After this, defendants came to know that even S.M.Bansali was also passed away. Sub- defendants contended that the plaintiffs never ever been in 10 O.S.872/ 22 possession of the suit schedule property. The plaintiffs have not approached this court with clean hands, thus their suit deserves to be dismissed. 6. By considering the pleadings of the parties this court had to frame the following ISSUES 1. Whether the plaintiff proves that they are in lawful possession of the suit schedule property as on the date of filing of suit? 2. Whether the plaintiff proves that the defendants who have no whatsoever right title interest over the suit schedule property were interfered with their possession and enjoyment of suit property, thus the relief of permanent injunction is requires to be granted to restrain them from interfering with the plaintiff's peaceful possession enjoyment of the suit scheduled property? 3. What order? 7. The plaintiffs in order to prove their case examined the plaintiff No.2 as PW1. They have also examined one of the witness 11 O.S.872/ 22 as PW2. The plaintiff in order to prove their case produced in all 12 documents which were marked as Exhibit P1 to 12. On the defendants side, it is the defendant No.8 on his behalf and also on behalf of the defendant Nos.5, 6, 7 and 9 examined himself as DW1 and produced in all 49 documents which were marked as Exhibit D1 to 49. 8. My findings to above said issues are as follows:- Issue no.1: As Affirmative; Issue no.2: As Affirmative; Issue no.3: As per final order for the following:- REASONS 9. ISSUE NOs.1 and 2: As these issues are interconnected, thus for avoiding the repetition of discussion on facts and evidence they are taken up for common discussion. The plaintiffs case is that they are the joint owners who have acquired the suit scheduled site bearing no. 18, formed out of converted land bearing Sy.No.66/1, renumbered as 284/11, CMC Khata No.5140/284/11, present Municipal No.18/9/2 of Halagevaderahalli village, Kengeri Hobli, Bangalore South Taluk. Presently the said layout is known as Giridhama Layout which is 12 O.S.872/ 22 the suit schedule property. Their contention is that the said site being carved in land bearing survey number 66/1 of Halagevadera Halli village, Kengeri Hobli, Bangalore South Taluk. The said land has been acquired by one G.H.Bangera under the registered sale deed dated 16th April 1971, which is the land measuring 2 acres 4 Guntas of said village and he got the land converted for non- agricultural purpose, vide order dated 25th April 1990. At the outset, this court at this stage itself would want to clarify the fact that the acquisition of above said land by Sri, G.H.Bangera, conversion of land for a non-agriculture purpose are an undisputed fact. Then the defendant Nos.1 to 4 who claims to be the sons of said G.H.Bangera and defendant Nos.5 to 9 who are claiming that they are the grandchildren of late G.H.Bangera have categorically admitted the said fact. 10. The next question and the next point would be that the plaintiff contended that said G.H. Bangera after getting the land conversion for non-agricultural purpose executed the GPA in favor of one S.M. Bansali to form the layout and sell the sites carved therein. Accordingly said Bansali, after forming the layout, sold the suit site in favour of one Smt.R.Sumitra under the Registered sale 13 O.S.872/ 22 deed dated 3rd July 1997. To substantiate the said fact, the plaintiffs have produced the certified copy of said sale deed as per Exhibit P1 and after purchasing the said site, said Sumitra.R allegedly got the katha which is as per Exhibit P2 from then Raja Rajeshwari CMC and said R.Sumitra sold the suit schedule property in favor of one Smt. Yamuna as per Exhibit P3, which is the registered sale deed dated 9th February 2004 who in turn sold the said property in favour of plaintiffs herein under a registered sale deed dated 27th January 2011. Thereafter, the plaintiff claims to have got katha and are paying taxes regularly. To that aspect, the plaintiffs have produced the original sale deed as per Exhibit P4 dated 27th January 2011, Katha as per Exhibit P5, Tax paid receipts as per Exhibit P6. 11. But the defendants have disputed all these facts on the grounds that, Firstly, they have denied that Sri G.H. Bangera had not at all executed any GPA in favor of S.M. Bansali. The said GPA is a created document, based on which one S.M.Bansali had executed the sale did in favor of one Smt. Sumitra as per exhibit P1 who has got the katha as per exhibit P2 and who sold the said property in favor of Smt. Yamuna as per Exhibit P3 and who in 14 O.S.872/ 22 turn sold the same in favor of plaintiff's hearin as per Exhibit P4, thus they alleged that those documents have no sanctity, as they are all by virtue of created documents. But the defendants have not challenged the said sale deeds before any court of law. Even this court made no efforts to examine the validity of the said sale deeds on whether said G.H. Bangera had executed any G.P.A. in favour of his agent Mr. S.M. Bansali, whether he in turn by carving the sites hold the same in favour of Smt.Sumitra, in turn sold the same in favour of Yamuna who in turn sold the same in favour of plaintiffs herein, as the suit is before this court is for bare injunction. As the plaintiffs are before this court claiming that after acquiring the said property they are in joint possession of suit schedule property, it is their specific case that after obtaining the building plan and license from the corporation which is as per Exhibit P8, P9 and P10. When they have started to put up the construction in the schedule property, surprisingly all of a sudden on 1st February 2022, the defendants herein came near the suit schedule properties shown the plaint copy in OS.No.6907/2012 filed against one Sumithra.R, who is the vendors vendor of the property. 15 O.S.872/22 12. In the case in hand, the defendants have disputed the fact that G. H. Bangera had not at all executed any GPA in favour of Mr. S.M.Bansali, thus who had no right to execute the sale deed of suit schedule property in favor of R. Sumitra, who in turn sold the same to Yamuna, and in she selling the same in favor of the plaintiffs, thus they are not valid transfer. Further alleged that, they have created document on the ground that during the lifetime of G.H.Bangera, after purchasing the land bearing survey number 66/1, measuring 2 acre 4 guntas, got the mutation of the said property, got the land converted and he died intestate. In OS.No.5442/09 wherein the said suit ended in compromise, wherein the mother of the defendant numbers 5 to 9 herein has got 0.15.13.00 guntas of land in survey number 66/1. And the suit schedule site is situated within the land allotted in favor of mother of the defendant Nos.5 to 9 herein. Thus, it is the defendant Nos.5 to 9 claims that they are in possession of the suit schedule property. But, wherein the plaintiff categorically contended that since from the date of purchase by Smt.R.Sumitra, further purchased by Yamuna and thereafter the plaintiffs, they were in continuous possession of the suit schedule property. The plaintiffs 16 O.S.872/ 22 with intent to put up construction in the said property claims to have got the license and plan as per Exhibit P9 and P10. In respect of said documents, there is no dispute. In fact, in this case, this court would requires to be adjudicated whether the plaintiffs are in possession by putting up of construction? Does this defendants have caused any interference, if so they are to be restrained from causing any interference to the construction carrying by the plaintiff and also they are to be restrained permanently from interfering with the plaintiff's possession & enjoyment of suit schedule property. 13. The plaintiffs to show their ownership produced the registered sale deed as per Exhibit P4, Katha as per Exhibit P5 and Tax paid receipts as per Exhibit P6 and the plan and license as per Ex.P9 and 10. In the evidence of the PW.1 he categorically deposed in the suit schedule property, after put up of construction he is residing, but though the defendants have chosen to cross examine the PW1, they have not elicited the fact that in the schedule property it is the defendants are in possession, and not by the plaintiffs. In fact, they have suggested that as on the date of filing of suit the plaintiffs were not in possession and the question of the 17 O.S.872/ 22 defendants causing an interference with the plaintiffs possession enjoyment of suit property do not arise. But there is no clarity as to does the plaintiff after filing of suit, does they completed the construction. But, to have clarification on the said facts, its relevant read the evidence of the D.W.No.1 deposed during the cross examination, wherein a question being posed stating that when do you got the knowledge that its the plaintiffs are in the possession of the suit schedule property? for which the D.W.No.1 answered that only after filing of the suit and after obtaining an interim order the plaintiffs have entered into the suit schedule property, then he came to know it is the plaintiffs who are in possession of the said property but he deposed that in the suit schedule property the plaintiffs during the pendency of the suit have put up the construction in the suit schedule property. For a specific question that does the land bearing survey number 66/1 is still a vacant land? From whom the parties who have put up the construction in land bearing Sy.No.66/1 have got purchased the sites,for which he answered that it is one Sharmila and Narayana reddy have formed the layout in Sy.No.66/2, the purchasers of sites in the said lands have inadvertently put up the construction in the land bearing survey number 66/1. He also answered to a 18 O.S.872/ 22 question that even the plaintiff who got purchased the site in Survey Number 66/2 but inadvertently have put up the construction in land bearing survey number 66/1 itself. 14. From the above, the plaintiffs have proved that after purchasing the suit schedule site under a sale deed at Exhibit P4, have got the katha, paying taxes, even by obtaining the plan and license as per exhibit P9 and 10, have put up the construction in the suit scheduled property and started reside. It appears when the plaintiffs have started to put up the construction, it is the defendants might have caused an interference as they have disputed the transaction done by one S.M.Bansali by claiming himself as G.P.A holder of the original landlord, Mr. G.H.Bangera, who is the father of the defendant Nos.1 to 4 and grandfather to the defendant Nos. 5 to 9 herein. 15. As the suit is one for a permanent injunction to restrain the defendant's herein from causing an interference with their construction activities and further sought the injunctive relief restraining the defendants from in any way causing an interference with the plaintiff's possession. As the plaintiffs have already completed the construction, restraining the defendants herein from 19 O.S.872/ 22 causing an interference with the construction activity is concerned, that relief become infructuous. The plaintiff is entitled for partial decree to restrain the defendant's herein from interfering with plaintiff's possession & enjoyment of suit schedule property. 16. As the defendants have contested the suit on various counts, in respect of the suit schedule property and also against the various purchasers who claims to have got purchased the sites carved in Survey No.66/1. In that event, if the defendants are not restrained from interfering with plaintiff's possession & enjoyment of suit schedule property that causes irreparable injury and hardship. 17. In fact, the plaintiff produced the Exhibit P-11, which is the certified copy of O.S.No.6974/1995 and the plaintiff in the said suit wherein one S. M. Bansali by claiming himself as G. P. A. holder of G. H. Bangera filed suit for injunction against one Narayana Reddy and two others in respect of land bearing survey number 66/1, to show that it is S.M.Bansali was the GPA holder. It is G.H.Bangera executed the said GPA in favor of said S.M.Bansali. He had the right to do transaction in respect of sites carved in land bearing survey number 66/1, but this court do not wish to give any 20 O.S.872/ 22 finding on the said document?. As the disputes filed by the defendants in respect of challenging the sale deeds of plaintiffs Vendor's Vendor are pending, in the said suit the validity and genuineness on whether G.H.BANGERA had executed GPA in favor of S.M. BANSALI, whether said Bansali had filed any suit like O.S. 6974/1995 and in fact whether the compromise entered between the defendants in O.S.No.5442/2009 is valid. Does that compromise is coming in any way in the right title interest of the plaintiff herein or the subject matter of said dispute. Though the defendants have produced documents in voluminous manner, this court would not want to discuss on the said document as the DW1 himself admitted that, it is the plaintiffs who are in possession and enjoyment by putting up of construction. Thus, until dispossessing them by way of due process, their possession is required to be protected by way of perpetual injunction as sought in the suit. Thus, I answer that the plaintiffs have proved that they are the lawful owners in possession as on the date of filing of suit and there was an interference from the defendants. Thus, they are to be restrained by way of perpetual injunction as sought in the suit. Thus, I answer the Issue Nos.1 and 2 as Affirmative. 21 O.S.872/22 18. ISSUE NO.3:- In view of my findings on Issue Nos.1 and 2 in the Affirmative, I proceed to pass the following: ORDER Suit of the plaintiff is decreed in part. By way of perpetual injunction, the defendants, their agents, anybody acting through them are hereby restrained permanently interfering with the plaintiff's possession and enjoyment of suit schedule property. Office is to draw the decree accordingly. (Dictated to the Stenographer, transcript thereof corrected, signed and then pronounced by me, in open Court, on this the 20 th day of January, 2025.) (GANGADHARA.K.N.) XII ADDL.CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE BENGALURU CITY. ANNEXURE I. List of witnesses examined on behalf of plaintiff side PW-1 : Mr. M.R.Rajagopal PW-2 : Mr. V. Srinivas. II. List of witnesses examined on behalf of Defendants side: 22 O.S.872/22 DW-1 : Sri. Rajesh.H III. Ex.P.7 Form no.15 for the period 01.04.2004 to 29.06.2020 Ex.P.8 License fee receipt Ex.P.9 Building license in 2 pages Ex.P.10 Building plan Ex.P.11 Certified copy of the order sheet in O.S 6974/1995 Ex.P.12 Certified copy of the plaint and valuation slip in O.S 6974/95 together marked. 23 O.S.872/22 IV. Ex.D.29 Certified copy of conversion order. Ex.D.30 Certified copy of letter dt 18.06.2002 Ex.D.31 Certified copy of 48 pg of RTC's pertaining to Sy.No. 66/1 Ex.D.32 Certified copy of the Sy.skech of 66 Ex.D.33 Certified copy of Judgment in O.S 6910/12 Ex.D.34 Certified copy of the Judgment in O.S 7619/12 Ex.D.35 Certified copy of the Judgment and Decree in O.S 6487/12 Ex.D.36 Certified copy of the Judgment and Decree in O.S 6534/11 Ex.D.37 Certified copy of the GPA dt 07.11.2017 Ex.D.38 Certified copy of the sale deed dt and 38(a) 15.04.1971 and typed copy of the sale deed dt 15.04.1971 Ex.D.39 Death Certificate of G.H.Bangera Ex.D.40 Death Certificate of M.Indira Ex.D.41 Certified copy of the memorandum of 26 O.S.872/ 22 settlement entered in O.S 5442/09 Ex.D.42 41 no.of RTC's pertaining to land bearing Sy.No. 66/1 of Halegeodeyaralli Village.
